mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques like PagedAttention and continuous batching. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and the complexity of configuration for specific use cases.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.17.0

```

> This adds the vLLM Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
Install the necessary CRDs for vLLM to function correctly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.17.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the vLLM CRDs.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.17.0

```

> This installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates an example vLLM instance to start serving models.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> This creates a vLLM instance named 'example-vllm'.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM instance
steps.description:
Check the status of the vLLM instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllms -n vllm

```

> This command lists all vLLM instances in the 'vllm' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM installation and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any necessary data. This process includes draining the existing pods, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.17.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get vllms -n vllm

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node hosting vLLM.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to allow scheduling again.
> Verify that the vLLM instance is running correctly after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Helm chart may not be installed or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Helm chart is installed and the correct namespace is used.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create VLLM instance'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD for vLLM may not be installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD using the command: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.17.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM instance may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource requests/limits in the vLLM CR and redeploy.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not be available.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and tag in the vLLM CR and ensure it is correct.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new models and improved PagedAttention performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM instance listed with a status of 'Running' when executing: kubectl get vllms -n vllm.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with the specified resources and the necessary tools installed.
